title: Authentication - API Keys
content:
All API requests require authentication via an API key passed in the X-API-Key header. You can generate API keys from the Developer Dashboard under Settings > API Keys.

There are two types of keys:

1. Test keys (prefixed with sk_test_) — use these in development. They interact with sandbox data and never trigger real transactions.

2. Live keys (prefixed with sk_live_) — use these in production. All requests with live keys affect real data.

Example request with authentication:

curl -X GET https://api.acmepay.com/v1/payments \
  -H "X-API-Key: sk_test_abc123def456"

If the key is missing or invalid, the API returns HTTP 401 with the body: {"error": "unauthorized", "message": "Invalid or missing API key."}.

Rate limits: Test keys are limited to 100 requests per minute. Live keys allow 1,000 requests per minute. Exceeding the limit returns HTTP 429.

title: Payments - Create a Payment
content:
To create a payment, send a POST request to /v1/payments with a JSON body.

Endpoint: POST https://api.acmepay.com/v1/payments

Required fields:
- amount (integer): Amount in the smallest currency unit (e.g., cents for USD). Must be greater than 0.
- currency (string): Three-letter ISO 4217 code (e.g., "usd", "inr", "eur").
- customer_id (string): The ID of the customer being charged.

Optional fields:
- description (string): A human-readable description of the payment.
- metadata (object): Key-value pairs for your own record-keeping.
- idempotency_key (string): A unique string to prevent duplicate charges.

Example request:

curl -X POST https://api.acmepay.com/v1/payments \
  -H "X-API-Key: sk_test_abc123def456" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"amount": 5000, "currency": "usd", "customer_id": "cus_12345", "description": "Order #1042"}'

Example response (HTTP 201):

{"id": "pay_98765", "status": "pending", "amount": 5000, "currency": "usd", "customer_id": "cus_12345", "created_at": "2026-03-01T10:30:00Z"}

Possible errors:
- 400 Bad Request: Missing required field or invalid currency code.
- 402 Payment Failed: The customer's payment method was declined.
- 409 Conflict: A payment with the same idempotency_key already exists.

title: Payments - Retrieve and List Payments
content:
Retrieve a single payment by its ID:

GET https://api.acmepay.com/v1/payments/{payment_id}

Example:

curl -X GET https://api.acmepay.com/v1/payments/pay_98765 \
  -H "X-API-Key: sk_test_abc123def456"

Response (HTTP 200):

{"id": "pay_98765", "status": "completed", "amount": 5000, "currency": "usd", "customer_id": "cus_12345", "created_at": "2026-03-01T10:30:00Z", "completed_at": "2026-03-01T10:30:05Z"}

List all payments with optional filters:

GET https://api.acmepay.com/v1/payments

Query parameters:
- customer_id (string): Filter by customer.
- status (string): Filter by status — one of "pending", "completed", "failed", "refunded".
- created_after (ISO 8601 datetime): Only return payments created after this time.
- created_before (ISO 8601 datetime): Only return payments created before this time.
- limit (integer, default 20, max 100): Number of results per page.
- offset (integer, default 0): Pagination offset.

Example:

curl -X GET "https://api.acmepay.com/v1/payments?customer_id=cus_12345&status=completed&limit=10" \
  -H "X-API-Key: sk_test_abc123def456"

The response includes a "data" array of payment objects and a "total_count" field.

title: Payments - Refund a Payment
content:
To refund a completed payment, send a POST request to /v1/payments/{payment_id}/refund.

Endpoint: POST https://api.acmepay.com/v1/payments/{payment_id}/refund

Optional fields:
- amount (integer): Partial refund amount in smallest currency unit. If omitted, a full refund is issued.
- reason (string): One of "duplicate", "fraudulent", "requested_by_customer".

Example (full refund):

curl -X POST https://api.acmepay.com/v1/payments/pay_98765/refund \
  -H "X-API-Key: sk_test_abc123def456" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"reason": "requested_by_customer"}'

Example (partial refund of $20.00):

curl -X POST https://api.acmepay.com/v1/payments/pay_98765/refund \
  -H "X-API-Key: sk_test_abc123def456" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"amount": 2000, "reason": "duplicate"}'

Response (HTTP 200):

{"id": "ref_55555", "payment_id": "pay_98765", "amount": 5000, "status": "refunded", "reason": "requested_by_customer", "created_at": "2026-03-04T12:00:00Z"}

Errors:
- 404 Not Found: Payment ID does not exist.
- 400 Bad Request: Payment is not in "completed" status, or refund amount exceeds original.
- 409 Conflict: Payment has already been fully refunded.

title: Customers - Create and Manage Customers
content:
Create a customer record before charging them.

Endpoint: POST https://api.acmepay.com/v1/customers

Required fields:
- email (string): Customer's email address. Must be unique.

Optional fields:
- name (string): Customer's full name.
- phone (string): Phone number in E.164 format (e.g., "+919876543210").
- metadata (object): Arbitrary key-value pairs.

Example:

curl -X POST https://api.acmepay.com/v1/customers \
  -H "X-API-Key: sk_test_abc123def456" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"email": "ravi@example.com", "name": "Ravi Kumar", "phone": "+919876543210"}'

Response (HTTP 201):

{"id": "cus_12345", "email": "ravi@example.com", "name": "Ravi Kumar", "phone": "+919876543210", "created_at": "2026-03-01T09:00:00Z"}

Update a customer: PATCH https://api.acmepay.com/v1/customers/{customer_id} — pass only the fields you want to change.

Delete a customer: DELETE https://api.acmepay.com/v1/customers/{customer_id} — returns HTTP 204. Deleting a customer does not cancel pending payments.

List customers: GET https://api.acmepay.com/v1/customers?limit=20&offset=0

title: Webhooks - Event Notifications
content:
AcmePay sends webhook events to your server when important things happen (e.g., a payment completes, a refund is issued).

Setup: Register your webhook URL in the Developer Dashboard under Settings > Webhooks. You can also use the API:

POST https://api.acmepay.com/v1/webhooks

{"url": "https://yourserver.com/webhooks/acmepay", "events": ["payment.completed", "payment.failed", "refund.created"]}

Event types:
- payment.completed: A payment has been successfully processed.
- payment.failed: A payment attempt failed.
- refund.created: A refund was issued.
- customer.created: A new customer was registered.

Webhook payload example:

{"event": "payment.completed", "data": {"id": "pay_98765", "amount": 5000, "currency": "usd", "customer_id": "cus_12345"}, "created_at": "2026-03-01T10:30:05Z"}

Security: Every webhook request includes an X-Webhook-Signature header. Verify it using your webhook secret (found in the Dashboard) with HMAC-SHA256 to ensure the request is genuine.

Retry policy: If your endpoint returns a non-2xx status, AcmePay retries up to 5 times with exponential backoff (1 min, 5 min, 30 min, 2 hours, 24 hours).

title: Error Handling and Status Codes
content:
All API errors follow a consistent format:

{"error": "error_code", "message": "Human-readable explanation.", "details": {}}

Common HTTP status codes:

- 200 OK: Request succeeded.
- 201 Created: Resource was created successfully.
- 204 No Content: Resource was deleted successfully.
- 400 Bad Request: The request body is malformed or missing required fields. Check the "details" object for field-level errors.
- 401 Unauthorized: API key is missing or invalid.
- 402 Payment Failed: The payment could not be processed (e.g., card declined).
- 404 Not Found: The requested resource does not exist.
- 409 Conflict: A resource with the same unique constraint already exists (e.g., duplicate idempotency_key or email).
- 429 Too Many Requests: You have exceeded the rate limit. Retry after the number of seconds specified in the Retry-After header.
- 500 Internal Server Error: Something went wrong on our end. If this persists, contact support@acmepay.com.

Best practices:
- Always check the HTTP status code before parsing the response body.
- Implement exponential backoff for 429 and 500 errors.
- Log the full error response for debugging.
